name: Ofennudle (Rohrnudle)
recipeInstructions:
Yeast dough:
Dissolve the yeast and some sugar in a small amount of warmed milk. Place the flour on a pastry board. Make a well in the center of the mound, and pour the yeast mixture into it. Let rest for about half an hour. Then add the remaining ingredients - sugar, eggs, salt, butter, and milk. Knead to mix well, and beat until the dough forms bubbles. Cover the dough with an inverted bowl and let rise in a warm place for 1 1/2 hours. Then cut off lumps of dough, and let those rise again on a floured pastry board for another half hour. With your hands, stretch each piece of dough and cover with fruit (one kind only). If using apples or damson plums, dust the fruit with cinnamon and sugar, otherwise just with sugar. Fold the dough over to enclose the fruit and arrange the dumplings in a baking dish with a buttered bottom (3 1/2 tablespoons butter per dumpling). Bake at medium heat for 35 to 40 minutes until light brown. Remove from oven and turn off on a board for cooling. Brush with melted butter shortly before serving.
Serves 4.
From: D'SCHWAEBISCH' KUCHE' by Aegidius Kolb and Leonhard Lidel, Allgaeuer Zeitungsverlag, Kempten. 1976 (Translation/Conversion: Karin Brewer)
